site.seoTitle: Jazhikho | Game Developer and Technical Designer
site.heroSummary: I make games, tools, and design experiments centered on procedural generation, technical systems, and reflective play.
site.heroPanelText: My work tends to live where systems design, worldbuilding, and thoughtful player experience overlap.
site.shortBio: I am a game developer and research-oriented creator focused on game systems, procedural generation, technical design, and reflective play.
aboutParagraphs: My public work includes playable games, atmospheric prototypes, and developer-facing tools. Across all of it, the common thread is pretty simple: I like building technical systems that support tone, pacing, clarity, and meaning instead of existing just to be complicated.

A lot of what I do sits around procedural worldbuilding, tools development, and systems thinking, with a technical background in C#, Godot, Unity, Python, and UI and UX-aware design. I also like treating research and analytical writing as part of development rather than something that only happens after the fact.

When AI-assisted workflows are part of the process, I approach them intentionally. I care about provenance, clear boundaries, and staying accountable for the final work, so the focus stays on what I am making, why I am making it, and how the tools fit into that.

projects.title: StarGen
projects.shortPitch: Procedurally generated galaxy and star-system tooling for automated worldbuilding.
projects.description: I built StarGen in Godot as a worldbuilding tool for generating galaxies, star systems, and celestial objects with seeded determinism, 3D viewers, and repeatable results.
projects.longDescription: StarGen is where a lot of my procedural worldbuilding interests come together. It treats generation as a design tool I can return to, inspect, and expand rather than a one-off gimmick.

projects.title: Project Harvest
projects.shortPitch: A first-person psychological horror roguelite about identity, complicity, and how players treat the bodies they control.
projects.description: I built Project Harvest in Godot as a psychological horror roguelite that uses repetition, atmosphere, and player choice to keep the relationship between player and avatar front and center.
projects.longDescription: Project Harvest is one of the clearest examples of how I like to approach reflective design. The structure, failures, and tone all work together to ask what responsibility we carry inside a game system.

projects.title: Relationship Overhead Tool
projects.shortPitch: A relationship simulation sandbox for testing whether nuanced social systems can remain computationally lightweight.
projects.description: I made this as a simulation and benchmarking tool for testing whether a nuanced relationship system could stay readable and computationally lightweight at the same time.
projects.longDescription: This project works as both a systems sandbox and a proof of concept. It is my way of exploring social simulation with more texture than a single relationship meter, without letting the overhead get out of hand.

projects.title: Loot, Luck & Liability
projects.shortPitch: A browser roguelite about risky runs, cursed treasure, and luck as both mechanic and punchline.
projects.description: I built this browser roguelite around fast runs, light progression, and a playful tone that still leaves room for solid systems work under the hood.
projects.longDescription: Loot, Luck & Liability let me lean into humor, quick iteration, and localization while still building a game with clear progression and readable moving parts.

projects.title: Private Eye Pierce & The Ink Spill
projects.shortPitch: A comic-noir point-and-click adventure where grayscale routine gives way to surreal color and mystery.
projects.description: This collaborative Unity prototype focuses on atmosphere, character, and story-driven puzzle design more than raw difficulty.
projects.longDescription: What I like most about this project is its tone. It uses environmental storytelling, monochrome contrast, and small surreal details to build a strong identity without overcomplicating the experience.

projects.title: Warp Engineer
projects.shortPitch: A browser puzzle about stabilizing a warp bubble by tuning harmonics under pressure.
projects.description: This is a systems-tuning prototype about stabilizing a warp bubble by managing harmonics, coherence, and pressure under changing conditions.
projects.longDescription: Warp Engineer is a small project, but it lines up with a lot of what I care about: readable feedback, technical framing, and being honest about process and provenance when AI tools are part of the workflow.
projects.role: Concept direction and public release ownership

projects.title: Manual Override
projects.shortPitch: An unfinished experimental prototype about what happens when trust in AI systems goes too far.
projects.description: I built this jam prototype around AI risk, misalignment, and what can happen when people hand off too much judgment to automated systems.
projects.longDescription: Manual Override is rough around the edges by design and circumstance, but it is also a direct expression of one of my recurring interests: how technical systems shape trust, control, and responsibility.

research.intro: A lot of my research interests show up through systems experiments, reflective design questions, and procedural-development work that makes its way into public prototypes and releases.
themes: Using procedural worldbuilding as a repeatable design and production tool.
Building systems-driven structures that still stay readable to players and collaborators.
Exploring reflective and eudaimonic design questions through playable work.
Treating implementation and analysis as part of the same development practice.
Thinking carefully about AI-assisted workflows, provenance, bounded use, and accountability.

emptyState.title: Right now, the research side of my work is mostly represented through projects and prototypes.
emptyState.body: Formal talks, papers, and publications can be added here as more of that work becomes public.
